question: Among Washington counties with low-income student enrollment between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22, which had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in 2021-22 and are not the most populous county in the state? List the county names in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2018-19, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county and calculating Low-Income percentage (Low-Income / All Students * 100), the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: Island (39.4%), King (34.7%), Kitsap (39.2%), San Juan (37.8%), Snohomish (39.6%), and Whitman (37.6%).
1.subquestion: Which Washington counties had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22? (Filter: OrganizationLevel=District; GradeLevel=All Grades; group by County; Low-Income% = Low-Income / All Students * 100; keep 32-40%.)

2.fact: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2019-20, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county, the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: Island (38.7%), King (34.2%), Kitsap (38.3%), San Juan (37.6%), Snohomish (39.3%), and Whitman (37.7%).
2.subquestion: Which Washington counties had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22? (Filter: OrganizationLevel=District; GradeLevel=All Grades; group by County; Low-Income% = Low-Income / All Students * 100; keep 32-40%.)

3.fact: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2020-21, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county, the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: Island (36.4%), King (34.1%), Kitsap (35.1%), San Juan (34.6%), Snohomish (38.3%), Thurston (39.4%), and Whitman (34.7%).
3.subquestion: Which Washington counties had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22? (Filter: OrganizationLevel=District; GradeLevel=All Grades; group by County; Low-Income% = Low-Income / All Students * 100; keep 32-40%.)

4.fact: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2021-22, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county, the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: King (35.9%), Kitsap (38.0%), San Juan (38.2%), and Whitman (39.3%). Note: Island had 40.3% and Snohomish had 41.8%, both exceeding the 40% threshold.
4.subquestion: Which Washington counties had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22? (Filter: OrganizationLevel=District; GradeLevel=All Grades; group by County; Low-Income% = Low-Income / All Students * 100; keep 32-40%.)

5.fact: According to the Washington Report Card Assessment data for 2021-22, averaging district-level PercentMetStandard for Math (All Students, All Grades) by county: King had 43.5%, Kitsap had 44.4%, San Juan had 35.2%, and Whitman had 41.4%. Counties with district-average Math proficiency above 40% among hop 1 result: King, Kitsap, Whitman.
5.subquestion: Among Washington counties with low-income student enrollment between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22 (King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman), which had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in 2021-22?

6.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for King County, Washington, 'The population was 2,269,675 in the 2020 census, making it the most populous county in Washington.'
6.subquestion: Among the counties from the low-income 2018-19 through 2021-22 intersection (King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman), which are NOT the most populous in Washington?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: 2018-19 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 2: 2019-20 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 3: 2020-21 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Thurston, Whitman}
Node 4: 2021-22 Low-Income 32-40% -> {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman}
HOP 2 (d=2, data.gov + Wikipedia - nodes 5-6 depend on hop 1):
Node 5: 2021-22 Math proficiency >40% -> King, Kitsap, Whitman
Node 6: King is the most populous county -> exclude King
